codeReview.summary: Well-implemented feature with comprehensive test coverage. Both `werk move` and `werk note` commands follow established patterns, properly handle edge cases, and fulfill all validation assertions (VAL-CRUD-019 through VAL-CRUD-024). Cycle prevention uses Forest::descendants correctly. Workspace notes use a pragmatic sentinel ID approach.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `Move` command enum variant uses `parent: Option<String>` parameter naming which is clear, but the doc comment could explicitly mention that omitting --parent makes the tension a root. Currently reads 'New parent ID (omit to make root)' which is accurate.

Cycle prevention pattern using Forest::descendants is effective and should be documented as the canonical approach for hierarchical integrity checks. The implementation correctly builds a HashSet of descendant IDs for O(1) lookup.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: werk-cli/src/main.rs:818-830: Uses forest.descendants() + HashSet for cycle detection

sharedStateObservations.observation: Workspace-level notes use a sentinel tension ID 'WORKSPACE_NOTES' which is not a real tension but serves as an anchor for mutations. This is a pragmatic solution but should be documented in architecture notes if a more formal mechanism isn't introduced later.

Worker followed rust-cli-worker skill procedure correctly: wrote tests first (23 tests), implemented GREEN phase, passed clippy, verified manually. Handoff correctly references all fulfilled validation assertions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff shows 23 tests added covering all expectedBehavior items, all validators passed

summary: Excellent implementation of `werk move` and `werk note` commands. Move correctly reparents tensions with robust cycle prevention using Forest::descendants. Note supports both tension-specific annotations (including on resolved/released tensions) and general workspace notes via sentinel ID. All 23 integration tests pass, clippy is clean, and all 6 validation assertions (VAL-CRUD-019 through VAL-CRUD-024) are fulfilled. The code follows established patterns from prior commands and properly delegates to sd-core Store methods.
